text.title: OAuth 2.0 Flows and Scope Management
text.summary: Authorization Code + PKCE flow for web/mobile apps, Client Credentials for S2S, and scope-based permission enforcement.

rule.statement: Use Authorization Code + PKCE for all public clients (SPAs, mobile); never Implicit flow or Password grant — both are deprecated in OAuth 2.1.
rule.rationale: Implicit flow exposes tokens in the URL fragment (logged by proxies). Password grant defeats the purpose of OAuth (the app never sees the password is a false claim when it collects it directly).

rule.statement: Always verify the state parameter on the callback to prevent CSRF; verify the PKCE code_verifier to prevent code interception.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated OAuth callbacks routinely omit state and PKCE verification, making them vulnerable to CSRF and authorization code interception attacks.

example.code:
1. User clicks "Login with Provider" 2. Redirect to: https://auth.example.com/authorize? client_id=abc& redirect_uri=https://app.com/callback& response_type=code& scope=read:profile& state=RANDOM_CSRF_TOKEN& code_challenge=BASE64URL(SHA256(code_verifier))& code_challenge_method=S256
3. User authenticates and grants permission 4. Redirect to: https://app.com/callback?code=AUTH_CODE&state=RANDOM_CSRF_TOKEN (verify state matches stored value)
5. Exchange code for tokens: POST https://auth.example.com/token grant_type=authorization_code& code=AUTH_CODE& code_verifier=ORIGINAL_RANDOM_STRING& client_id=abc& redirect_uri=https://app.com/callback
example.note: Use authlib or oauthlib — never hand-roll the redirect dance.

rule.statement: Use Client Credentials grant for machine-to-machine; store client_secret in env or a secrets manager, never in source code.
rule.rationale: S2S tokens don't involve user context; a simple POST to the token endpoint with client credentials gives a short-lived access token scoped to the requested permissions.

rule.statement: Define scopes as a registry and enforce per-route via a policy layer; never scatter if user.role checks across handler code.
rule.rationale: Scattered role checks are easy to forget and hard to audit. A centralised scope checker raises 403 consistently and can be tested independently of business logic.
